title: Within-stint candidate ranking blended with calibrated classifier
summary: Official strategy analysis emphasizes that pit calls are local comparisons between stopping now, stopping one lap later, or reacting to a nearby rival.
rationale: Official strategy analysis emphasizes that pit calls are local comparisons between stopping now, stopping one lap later, or reacting to a nearby rival. A within-stint candidate ranker makes that comparison directly, which is closer to the operational decision than global row-wise classification.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race stint, build a query group over laps and train a ranking model so true stop-adjacent laps outrank neighboring laps, using CatBoost QueryCrossEntropy/QuerySoftMax or pairwise losses; convert ranks to scores and blend them out-of-fold with the main probabilistic classifier, then calibrate the blend with fold-safe sigmoid or isotonic scaling.
expected_effect: Should improve relative ordering inside the narrow pit window while the classifier preserves global probability structure.
risk: This overlaps conceptually with earlier ranking experiments, so it must be implemented as a strict within-stint candidate ranker rather than a generic race-level ranker; poor OOF blending can erase gains.
